title: Is Eva soap opera ish
wikitext:
[]: Is Eva soap opera-ish?             Quote Post by GAP » Sat Feb 07, 2009 1:11 pm After watching Gundam for a little, I come to realize that a large majority ofd real/super robot shows are somewhat soap opera-ish or more specifically they are space operas. The stories usually deal with wars against some force, organization, nations and what not and the heroes usually win (I hadn't seen that many robot shows so I am being general about this). If eva is a deconstruction of this, does that mean that it attempted to break the same mold as though shows in its attempt to include all the Freudian references and psychological emotional disorders? That is the one thing that amazes me about the giant mechas, that it focuses on the people fighting the battles and not on the civilians or groups but I don't know if I can assume that Eva is the same way considering that all the characters are emotional wrecks that are just trying to survive.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Feb 07, 2009 2:14 pm http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Serial_%28radio_and_television%29

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Sun Feb 08, 2009 10:11 am LoL, how do you pronounce "opera-ish" ??

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Feb 08, 2009 11:25 am Q. Is Eva soap opera-ish? A. Yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Feb 08, 2009 12:19 pm simon wrote: LoL, how do you pronounce "opera-ish" ??

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Feb 08, 2009 12:21 pm I don't see how EVA can be like a Soap Opera. The Soap Opera's I've seen were boring, dull, bad acting, and I could not watch it for more than 3 to 5 minutes.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Feb 08, 2009 12:22 pm And it takes a week of episodes to get through 1 day.

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Sun Feb 08, 2009 12:36 pm An interesting question, by American standards it really be a sort of mini series (then again a lot of anime would fall under this category). Is it soap opera-ish, I mean I guess you can argue that, but under that argument every tv show and anime that isn't made up purely of one shots is soap opera-ish. Though I would personally say its not a soap opera because from what I have seen, the key ingredient of a soap is that it purposely has no ending, where as Eva has one.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sun Feb 08, 2009 6:20 pm Never thought I'd see the day when Evangelion fans complain that "it's too soap".

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sun Feb 08, 2009 6:35 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: boring, dull, bad acting, and I could not watch it for more than 3 to 5 minutes.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Feb 09, 2009 11:49 am dictionary.com wrote: Soap Opera a radio or television series depicting the interconnected lives of many characters often in a sentimental, melodramatic way. Space Opera any melodramatic literature, motion picture, or television program set in outer space

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Feb 09, 2009 8:01 pm No, Eva isn't particularly soap opera-ish. Things that would have happend if Eva were a soap opera: 1. Shinji would have chosen Rei over Asuka and only then would he have discovered she was his mother's clone (cue dramatic music). 2. In ep. 21 there would have been a scene where Gendo seduced Ritsuko, and then she would have discovered he was also doing her mother (cue dramatic music). They would have played up a lot more the fact that he ended up betraying them both . 3. Langley would have been a lot more sinister; there would have been a scene with him and Doctor-lady conspiring to trap Kyoko in Unit-02 (cue sinister music). 4. There wouldn't have been any phone call from Germany scene because Asuka would have secretly killed Langley and Doctor-lady for the above, and made it look like an accident. This revelation would have been the highlight of Asuka's section of ep. 25 (cue dramatic music). 5. Misato's intentions in ep. 23 would have been made more explicit. She would have come into Shinji's room in a sheer neglige (cue sexy music, shifting to dramatic when he rejects her). ect.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Mon Feb 09, 2009 8:34 pm ^Yeah, guess that pretty much sums it up.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Tue Feb 10, 2009 2:17 am thewayneic has won the topic. *cue dramatic music*

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue Feb 10, 2009 7:05 am I suppose I'll have to repost my repost of an explanation of Evangelion. Me, in a far off, forgotten thread wrote: Its not a nice little subplot, its the backbone of the entire show. See, Gendo is infertile, so Fuyutsuki brought in his old sensei to father his wife's child, because he knew they had sort of hit it off in their college days. So, Gendo played the part of father, but kept Fuyutuski around so he would know his son. Then after Yui was gone, he sent Shinji away, because he developed a fierce anger that he had nothing of his own from Yui except memories. This would explain two things, why Fuyutsuki is nicer to Shinji than Gendo, and Unit-01 having a horn (Gendo was really compensating for something) Evangelion is really just a trashy dark romance story with sci-fi elements.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Feb 10, 2009 7:37 am thewayneiac wrote: 1. Shinji would have chosen Rei over Asuka and only then would he have discovered she was his mother's clone (cue dramatic music).

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Feb 10, 2009 10:45 am honsou wrote: Though I would personally say its not a soap opera because from what I have seen, the key ingredient of a soap is that it purposely has no ending, where as Eva has one.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 10, 2009 12:30 pm Zaiku: Seconded! Stifling laughs in a study hall is hard. I could go for more of that stuff; hilarious. CC: I actually thought of the "Fuyutsuki = Shinji's father" thing the second I finished reading Wayne's post. (But wait. "...so Fuyutsuki brought in his old sensei..."???) Is Soap-Fuyutsuki the father of Ritsuko, as well...?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Tue Feb 10, 2009 12:39 pm Maya would be Ritsuko's daughter, and in a particularly heated argument this would occur: Maya: Leave me alone! YOUR NOT MY MOTHER! Ritsuko: YES I AM!!! Dun, dun, dun, da-da-dadun! Cue credits.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 10, 2009 12:45 pm Ritsuko must have hit menarche at a disastrously tender age, then.

The stories usually deal with wars against some force, organization, nations and what not and the heroes usually win (I hadn't seen that many robot shows so I am being general a
